finding.summary: The track is heavily dominated by silence (92% dead air) and shows an extreme discrepancy between true peak (-5.5 dBFS) and average levels (RMS -55 dBFS), making any tonal or dynamic diagnosis unreliable.
finding.probable_cause: The capture likely contains only short, loud bursts amid long stretches of silence, so level statistics are diluted by the silence and do not represent the track's active content.
finding.confidence_reason: Confidence is capped at low because audio.silence_fraction is 0.920.

evidence_refs.description: Fraction of capture that is dead air, 0.92.
RMS level of -55 dBFS indicates extremely low average level due to silence.

proposal.reason: Audio silence fraction is 0.92, far above the acceptable threshold for a safe diagnosis. No reliable move can be proposed.
